subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Components
- **110 cards** — Word cards used to build the session deck.
- **7 easels** — Reusable writing stands for clues and active-word display.
- **7 erasable felt markers** — One marker per easel in full player count.
- **1 rulebook** — Official rules and variant reference.

#### Session Build (Per Game)
- **13 cards used** — Shuffle and draw exactly 13 cards for this game.
- **Unused cards** — Return all remaining cards to the box.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
--- 3 Players ---
- **Easels per player** — Each player takes 2 easels.
- **Clues per player** — Each player writes 2 clues during clue selection.
- **Deck size** — Still draw 13 cards for the session.

--- 4-7 Players ---
- **Easels per player** — Each player takes 1 easel.
- **Markers per player** — Each player takes 1 erasable felt marker.
- **Deck size** — Shuffle and draw 13 cards face down in the center.

#### Shared Setup Rules
- **Privacy** — Write clues where others cannot see before reveal.
- **Erase between turns** — Clear old clues before the next round.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **First active player** — Choose randomly.
- **Card handling** — Active player draws top card without looking.
- **Display method** — Active player places card in easel support so others can read all 5 words.
- **Word selection** — Active player chooses a number from 1 to 5.
- **Fallback** — If clue-givers do not know that word, active player chooses a different number.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose mystery word** — Active player presents card and selects word number (1-5).
2. **Write clues** — Non-active players secretly write clue word(s) with no communication.
3. **Compare clues** — Active player closes eyes; clue-givers reveal to each other and cancel invalid/duplicate clues.
4. **Make one guess** — Active player opens eyes and gives exactly one guess from remaining clues.
5. **Resolve result** — Apply success, failure, skip, or all-cancelled outcome.
6. **Rotate active player** — Player to the left becomes new active player.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Clue Writing Rules
- **Single-word rule** — Each clue must be one word.
- **Allowed word forms** — Digits, numbers, acronyms, onomatopoeia, and special characters are allowed as words.
- **No table talk** — Players may not coordinate while writing clues.

#### Duplicate Cancellation
- **Exact duplicates** — Identical clues are cancelled.
- **Family variants** — Plurals, gender forms, and spelling variants are treated as duplicates and cancelled.
- **Hidden cancelled clues** — Tip easel down to hide cancelled clue.

#### Invalid Clues
- **Disguised target** — Mystery word spelled differently is invalid.
- **Translated target** — Mystery word in another language is invalid.
- **Same family as target** — Derivative word from same family is invalid.
- **Invented word** — Made-up words are invalid.
- **Phonetic trap** — Phonetically matching forms listed as invalid by rules are invalid.

#### Guess Resolution
- **Guess limit** — Active player gets exactly one guess.
- **Correct guess** — Place card face up in success area.
- **Wrong guess** — Return current card and top deck card to box.
- **Skip choice** — Return only current card to box.
- **All clues cancelled** — Return current card to box; go straight to end of turn.
- **Pronunciation allowance** — Same-pronunciation guess is valid.
- **Final-turn penalty** — Wrong guess on final turn also removes one previously successful card.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Variant
--- 3 Players ---
- **Double clue load** — Each player writes 2 clues instead of 1.
- **Duplicate rule unchanged** — Duplicate cancellation still applies across all written clues.

--- 4-7 Players ---
- **Standard clue load** — Each non-active player writes 1 clue.

#### Cooperative Core
- **Shared objective** — Team tries to maximize total successful words.
- **Design tension** — Best clues are both understandable and non-duplicating.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **End trigger** — Game ends when the 13-card deck is empty.

#### Score Table (Successful Cards)
- **13** — Perfect score! Can you do it again?
- **12** — Incredible! Your friends must be impressed!
- **11** — Awesome! That’s a score worth celebrating!
- **9-10** — Wow, not bad at all!
- **7-8** — You’re in the average. Can you do better?
- **4-6** — That’s a good start. Try again!
- **0-3** — Try again, and again, and again.

#### How Cards Change Final Score
- **Success gain** — Each correct guess adds 1 successful card.
- **Wrong guess loss** — Wrong guess removes current card plus top deck card.
- **Skip loss** — Skip removes current card.
- **All-cancelled loss** — If all clues are cancelled, current card is removed.
- **Final-turn extra loss** — Wrong final-turn guess removes one previously successful card.

#### Tie-Break Note
- **Co-op scoring** — Team shares one total score, so no competitive tie-breaker is used.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Avoid obvious first pick** — Mentally list the most obvious clue and do not use it.
- **Use second-layer clues** — Prefer clues by function, category, or context to reduce duplication.
- **Anchor early rounds** — In first 2-3 turns, choose broad but stable clue styles your group understands.
- **Pick known words** — As active player, choose a 1-5 word index others are likely to recognize.
- **Read survivor clues** — As guesser, treat rare surviving clues as stronger signals than obvious missing ones.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Obvious-clue spam** — Repeating obvious synonyms causes mass cancellations.
- **Invalid-family clues** — Using same-family words throws away clue slots.
- **Over-clever obscurity** — Unique clues that nobody understands are effectively blanks.
- **No adaptation** — Ignoring your group’s repeated clue habits increases collision rate.
- **Instant guessing** — Guessing too fast misses combined meaning from the remaining clue set.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Clarity vs uniqueness** — Choose between a safer but duplicative clue and a rarer clue with lower certainty.
- **Risk vs preservation** — Late game, decide whether to guess or skip to avoid larger penalty swings.
- **Specificity vs flexibility** — Pick narrow clues for precision or broader clues that survive cancellation more often.

metadata.notes: Primary source: Just One rulebook text provided in prompt, including setup, 4-phase turn flow, clue validity/cancellation rules, score table, and 3-player variant.
